content.full_text:
County Counsel of the County of Tulare

2900 W. Burrel — County Civic Center — Visalia, CA 93291
Telephone (209) 733-6263
FAX (209) 730-2597

June 25, 1990

Mark Moradomi, Esq.
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re: Request for Written Opinion

Dear Mr. Moradomi:

In reference to our telephone conversation of June 22, 1990, this office requests, on behalf of Supervisor Delores E. (Lorie) Magine, that the Fair Political Practices Commission render a written opinion as to whether a conflict of interest is presented under the Political Reform Act, under the following set of facts.

Mrs. Magine is currently a Supervisor of the County of Tulare. She is also a candidate for the office of Tulare County Treasurer/Tax Collector. She is running against the incumbent for that office. Mrs. Magine will, as a County Supervisor, have the opportunity to have input into and to vote regarding the budget for the office of Treasurer/Tax Collector. The question which has arisen is, given these circumstances, does Mrs. Magine have a legal conflict of interest in so voting?

We would appreciate your written response to our question as soon as the press of your business will allow. If we may provide any further information, please do not hesitate to contact this office.

Mark Moradomi, Esq.
June 25, 1990
Page 2

Thank you for your cooperation in this matter.

Very truly yours,

LITA O'NEILL BLATNER
County Counsel

By [signature]
Kathleen Bales-Lange
Principal Attorney

cc: Lorie Magine
